objective: Implement the planning and scope gatekeeper slice from epic #224 so governance-surface edits require an issue-backed canonical structured JSON plan, accepted review state, implementation-ready handoff, and declared execution scope before implementation or closeout proceeds.
scope_boundary: Strengthen `scripts/overlord/validate_structured_agent_cycle_plan.py` so governance-surface path changes trigger issue-branch and issue-specific structured-plan enforcement regardless of branch name.
Classify governance-surface paths covering repo rules, standards, agents, workflows, hooks, graphify, sweep/overlord scripts, registries, closeouts, cross-review artifacts, schemas, and wiki outputs.
Wire the governance-surface planning gate into reusable governance CI and the local code-write hook path.
Preserve existing execution-scope validation and add tests proving wrong checkout roots, dirty forbidden roots, and out-of-scope changes fail.
Document the new gate in feature, service, data dictionary, PDCAR, cross-review, and closeout artifacts.
out_of_scope: Implementing the always-on daemon, packet queue, launchd observers, or local model router from later #224 phases.
Replacing GitHub Issues as the canonical tracker.
Relaxing Society of Minds no-self-approval, alternate-family review, or tier evidence requirements.
Writing to the shared dirty main checkout.
Adding broad new schema engines or external dependencies when the repo-local Python validator already covers the contract.
research_summary: Issue #226 requires closing the governance-surface bypass where implementation could proceed on a non-issue branch or with only a Markdown PDCAR. Existing validators already cover canonical structured-plan shape and execution-scope root/branch/write-path assertions, but the structured-plan validator only required a plan for issue/riskfix branch names and did not inspect changed governance-surface paths. The implementation therefore extends the existing validator with a deterministic path classifier, issue-number extraction, changed-file input, implementation-ready handoff checks, and accepted alternate-review checks. CI writes the PR diff file list to a temp file and calls the same validator. The local code-write gate calls the same validator for the file being written, so interactive governance-surface writes fail early when no issue-backed plan/review/handoff exists. Unit tests cover governed and non-governed path classifications, issue-specific plan matching, review readiness, and the existing execution-scope dirty-root protections.

sprints.name: Classifier and Plan Gate
sprints.goal: Make changed governance-surface paths require issue-backed canonical planning and implementation-ready review state.
tasks: Add deterministic governance-surface file and prefix classification to the structured-plan validator.
Accept a newline-delimited changed-file list and branch name as validator inputs.
Require governance-surface changes to run on an issue branch with a matching `issue_number` structured plan.
Require matching plans to be approved and `implementation_ready` or `implementation_complete`.
Require required alternate-model review to be `accepted` or `accepted_with_followup` before implementation.
acceptance_criteria: A governance-surface edit on `main` or a non-issue branch fails with an actionable message.
A governance-surface edit on an issue branch without a matching structured JSON plan fails.
A Markdown PDCAR without the canonical JSON plan is insufficient for governed work.
An approved matching implementation-ready plan passes the governance-surface gate.

sprints.name: Hook and CI Wiring
sprints.goal: Run the same deterministic governance-surface gate in reusable CI and local write-time enforcement.
tasks: Persist CI changed-file lists before downstream categorization outputs are computed.
Run the structured-plan validator with governance-surface enforcement in `governance-check.yml`.
Invoke the validator from `hooks/code-write-gate.sh` for repo-local write paths.
Keep hook behavior fail-closed only for validator-confirmed governance-surface writes and otherwise degrade gracefully.
acceptance_criteria: Reusable governance CI blocks governance-surface changes missing issue/plan/review/handoff readiness.
The local hook blocks governance-surface writes before closeout when the plan gate is not satisfied.
Non-governance writes are not forced into issue-plan flow by this slice.

sprints.name: Scope Evidence and Documentation
sprints.goal: Keep #226 auditable through execution-scope tests, registries, PDCAR, cross-review, and closeout artifacts.
tasks: Retain and run execution-scope tests for wrong root, wrong branch, dirty forbidden roots, and out-of-scope paths.
Register the gate in feature, service, and data dictionary documents.
Run closeout graph/wiki refresh and record validation evidence.
acceptance_criteria: Unit tests cover governed and non-governed path classifications.
Execution-scope tests prove wrong checkout root and out-of-scope dirty paths fail locally.
Registry and closeout artifacts cite the implemented gate and validations.

specialist_reviews.focus: Validator behavior, CI/local hook wiring, and tests for issue-backed governance-surface planning enforcement.
specialist_reviews.summary: The implementation uses the existing structured-plan validator as the shared enforcement point instead of introducing a second policy engine. Scope checks remain in the existing execution-scope validator and tests.

alternate_model_review.summary: Alternate-family review required for Tier 1 governance enforcement. Findings are recorded in the #226 cross-review artifact and must be resolved or explicitly accepted before merge.

execution_handoff.approved_scope_summary: Implement #226 planning/scope gatekeeper only: structured-plan validator enforcement, local/CI wiring, focused tests, and governance documentation.
execution_handoff.next_execution_step: Run validator, hook, CI workflow syntax, execution-scope, compendium, graphify, and model-pin checks; then close out, open PR, and merge only after checks pass.

material_deviation_rules: If CI cannot provide changed files, fail with a missing changed-file input error instead of silently allowing governance-surface edits.
If a governance-surface path is disputed, classify it as governed until a later issue narrows the list with review.
If alternate-model review raises blocking findings, update the implementation and cross-review artifact before merge.
If the shared main checkout is dirty, do not write to it; prove forbidden-root behavior through tests and isolated worktree validation instead.
approved_by: operator instruction: proceed, loop through, no HITL
